name: HaloBeaconsModal
description: Self-contained modal that opens via its own trigger button to show three accessory feature cards across the top and a side-by-side product comparison table below.
notes: Use: When the page needs to introduce two related accessories (or product variants) and let the reader skim a feature-by-feature comparison without leaving the page. Avoid: Comparing more than two products (use a dedicated page); comparing a single product's specs (use a static spec list). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/shop.html#included-modal

name: HaloBeforeBuyTriptych
description: Three-up dark cards under a centered heading — each card is one of three layouts: a bulleted list, an intro + steps list, or a paragraph block. Card 1 optionally surfaces a 'See complete details' modal.
notes: Use: Pre-purchase reassurance section that condenses what's-in-the-box / next steps / plan info into one scannable triptych. Avoid: When more than three items need equal weight (use a grid), or when each card needs a hero image (use feature cards). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#details

name: HaloFeaturePillsShowcase
description: Product showcase with a stack of expandable feature pills on the left (one open at a time, accordion-style) and a large product image on the right; the first pill is a colors picker that swaps the product image when a swatch is selected.
notes: Use: PDP-style section that introduces a hero product through bite-sized features without committing to a long feature grid; ideal for one flagship SKU. Avoid: Comparing more than one product (use a comparison grid); on pages with no hero product photography. Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#meet-halo

name: HaloMembershipPlansModal
description: Self-contained pricing-plan comparison modal with a trigger button that opens a three-column plan grid (basic, popular, premium).
notes: Use: When a page references subscription/membership tiers and needs to surface a full feature comparison without leaving the page. Avoid: When the plan comparison should be flat on the page (use a static 3-column grid instead) or when there's only one plan. Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/shop.html#membership-modal

name: HaloPricingProductCard
description: Two-column pricing module: a large product photo on the left and a buy-card on the right with a 'On Sale' badge, price stack, color picker, feature checklist, primary CTA, and Affirm + guarantee microcopy.
notes: Use: End-of-page conversion section for a hero product (one SKU, multiple colorways). Pairs well after a feature showcase. Avoid: When pricing varies by configuration (size, plan tier) — use a configurator instead. Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#pricing

name: HaloProductConfigBuyStack
description: PDP right column: product title with trust badge, color picker (dispatches `halo:color-change` for sibling components), feature-icon grid, membership info, price, primary CTA, and a guarantee strip.
notes: Use: PDP buy stack — pair with HaloProductGallery on the left for a full PDP. The component is self-contained except for cart wiring (the CTA is a plain link by default). Avoid: Catalog grids (this is a per-SKU module); pages without product config (use a slim CTA card instead). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/shop.html#product-config

name: HaloProductGallery
description: Waterfall product gallery: a stack of lifestyle images followed by a colorway-specific hero photo that swaps when a sibling color picker dispatches a `halo:color-change` event on `window`.
notes: Use: PDP left column when the right column has a config/buy-stack with a color picker that should drive which colorway hero photo appears. Avoid: Single-colorway products (drop the colorway swap and use a static stack). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/shop.html#gallery

name: HaloFeatureFlipGrid
description: Horizontally-scrolling grid of large flip cards: each card shows a hero image + title on the front and a 3-D-flipped paragraph back on click.
notes: Use: Mid-page feature showcase when each feature has a strong product photo and a longer explanation worth a tap to reveal. Avoid: Mobile-first contexts where 3-D flip is awkward (the grid still scrolls horizontally but the flip feels heavy on a 5" screen). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#features

name: HaloHowItWorksGrid
description: Bento-grid of four steps explaining how the product works; each cell is a yellow circular icon, a heading, and a paragraph on a dark glass card.
notes: Use: After the hero on a product or category page, when you need to explain a multi-step setup or workflow in a glanceable way. Avoid: When steps are sequential and need order numbers (use a numbered timeline instead), or when explanations need illustrations rather than icons. Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#how-works

name: HaloPersonaGrid
description: Two-column grid of persona cards, each a full-bleed photo with a gradient overlay carrying a tag and a one-paragraph description.
notes: Use: Mid-funnel sections that segment the audience visually ('who is this for?') with lifestyle imagery. Avoid: When personas don't have strong hero-quality photography, or when fewer than 3 personas are needed. Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#who-is-for

name: HaloTabbedFAQ
description: Tabbed FAQ with multiple categories; each tab opens its own accordion list of questions where exactly one item is expanded at a time.
notes: Use: When the FAQ is large enough (10+ questions) that grouping by category makes the page scannable. A single-tab FAQ should just be a flat accordion. Avoid: When there are fewer than 5 questions per category (the tabs feel underweight); use a single accordion list instead. Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#faq

name: HaloMainNav
description: Top-of-page navigation bar: logo on the left, a row of links (each optionally with a hover dropdown) in the middle, account dropdown + primary CTA on the right. Mobile collapses to a hamburger that opens a slide-in sidebar mirroring the same links.
notes: Use: Default sitewide top nav for any page that needs both desktop and mobile navigation surfaces. Avoid: Application shells with a left rail (this is a top bar); single-page promo landers (use a slim CTA-only bar instead). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#nav

name: HaloPastGenBanner
description: Single full-bleed banner card with a product photo on the left and an overlay with pill label, two-line headline, price stack, and CTA on the right.
notes: Use: Surface a markdown or previous-generation SKU on a primary landing page without giving it its own full section. Avoid: When you have more than one offer (use a grid of cards), or when the offer needs more than ~12 words of body copy. Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#past-gen-section

name: HaloPromoBanner
description: Slim top-of-page banner that rotates between one or more short promo messages on a timer.
notes: Use: Sitewide announcement strip above the nav for time-sensitive offers, guarantees, or shipping notes. Avoid: When the message is permanent (use a static band instead) or when more than ~80 characters per message is needed. Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#promo-banner

name: HaloSiteFooter
description: Full sitewide footer: logo + social icon row across the top, a responsive multi-column link grid below, and a bottom legal strip with copyright, legal links, and an optional light/dark theme toggle.
notes: Use: Bottom-of-page footer for any sitewide layout. The column model is data-driven, so consumers control the number and content of columns. Avoid: Single-page-app dashboards where a footer would compete with workspace controls. Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#footer

name: HaloStickyAnchorNav
description: Slim sticky strip that appears at the top of the viewport once the user scrolls past the hero; shows a product name, in-page anchor links, an optional price stack, and a small CTA button.
notes: Use: Long single-product landing pages where the reader needs a persistent way to jump between sections and add to cart without scrolling back to the main nav. Avoid: Pages with fewer than 3 long-form sections (the nav has nothing to anchor); mobile-only pages (this control is desktop-first). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#section-nav

name: HaloAppFeatureCarousel
description: Wide horizontally-scrolling carousel of app-screenshot cards with desktop + mobile image variants, a title, and a paragraph caption per card.
notes: Use: Mid-page section that walks through features of a companion app or product surface, when each feature has its own screenshot. Avoid: When you only have a single image (use a static figure instead) or when screenshots are very small (these cards are wide). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#halo-app

name: HaloBlurCarousel
description: Horizontal carousel of tall image-and-caption tiles with prev/next nav and a viewport-aware boundary (the next button disables exactly when the last tile is fully visible).
notes: Use: Mid-page features overview when each feature has a strong photo (lifestyle or detail shot) and a one-line caption; works with 4–8 slides. Avoid: When features need rich descriptions (the captions are intentionally tight), or when you have fewer than 4 slides (use a static grid instead). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#blur-carousel-section

name: HaloHeroSplitVideo
description: Full-bleed dark hero with a background image, a left-aligned headline + subhead + primary CTA, and a right-aligned auto-playing video preview that links out to a full YouTube watch.
notes: Use: Top-of-page hero for product launches where you have both a great photo and a short hero video; mixes the emotional weight of a hero shot with a glanceable motion preview. Avoid: When you don't have video assets ready, or when the page is content-first (a static hero is calmer). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#hero

name: HaloVideoSpotlight
description: Tall full-bleed band with a left-aligned multi-line headline and a big round play button that opens a YouTube embed in a fullscreen lightbox.
notes: Use: Mid-page hand-off to a product video — when you want the headline to read like 'Watch X in action' and a single tap to open the player. Avoid: When the video should auto-play inline (use a hero with embedded video instead). Original source pointer: pages/Lander-v2-2026/mutation.js#watch-action-section

description: Color swatch selector and selected-color label
